title: Plan
body:
# MVP Plan: AI Manager Transition Coach

## Approach

Built a single-file Node.js CLI app (`coach.js`) that uses the Claude Sonnet 4-6 API to provide multi-turn, context-aware coaching for managers navigating AI transition.

**Why CLI first:** Gets end-to-end flow working immediately. No frontend build pipeline, no auth, no database — just the core coaching loop. Can be wrapped in a web UI later.

## Architecture

```
coach.js
  - System prompt: Coaching persona, focus areas, style guidelines
  - Conversation:  In-memory message history for context continuity
  - Streaming:     Real-time streamed responses (Anthropic SDK)
  - readline:      Node.js terminal I/O for the conversation loop
```

**Model:** `claude-sonnet-4-6`
**SDK:** `@anthropic-ai/sdk` v0.86.1
**Commit:** `e85edb8`

## System Prompt Design

The coaching persona is primed to:
- Ask clarifying questions before giving advice
- Give concrete, actionable guidance (not platitudes)
- Address 7 core focus areas: AI capabilities assessment, role impact analysis, AI literacy building, workflow redesign, team resistance management, ROI measurement, staying current
- Be honest about AI's real impact, not just reassuring

## Demo Instructions

```bash
# From the project directory
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key_here
npm start
```

Sample inputs to demo the coaching:
- "My team is resistant to using AI tools and I'm not sure how to get them on board."
- "I'm worried AI will make my role irrelevant. What should I focus on?"
- "We've been told to use AI to increase output by 30% but I don't know where to start."
- "I'm a middle manager at a 500-person company. We just got Copilot licenses but no one is using them."

## What Was Built

- `coach.js` — Main app (~150 lines), fully functional
- `package.json` — Project config with `npm start` script
- `README.md` — Setup, demo walkthrough, architecture docs
- `.gitignore` — Excludes node_modules

## What's Next (not in scope for MVP)

- Web interface (Express + simple HTML chat UI)
- Conversation persistence (save/resume sessions)
- Manager profile capture (role, industry, team size) to personalize from turn 1
- Evaluation framework to measure coaching quality
